action.reasoning: Observation: test_preprocess.py succeeded with FULL SEQUENTIAL PREPROCESS (language step merge after LanguageProcessorStep preserves 'observation' key + PIL 224x224 images under exact PI0 view names + padded 32D state/action + complementary_data/task). But predict.py remains truncated (incomplete _prepare_observation/_safe_preprocess) causing 'All image features are missing from the batch' -> fallback zero-action and 0.0 success_rate across all 20+ iters. RecordingEvaluator, PI0 load, delta bridge, chunking pattern, and mock test (success=1.0) are solid. Analysis: The exact working batch+sequential+merge pattern from diagnostic resolves the training-oriented pipeline mutation issue (LanguageProcessorStep returns partial dict; merge tokens/attention_mask/language while preserving full observation/images/state). Full non-truncated predict.py copying this pattern into _safe_preprocess + _prepare_observation (map 4-cam rgb[0,2,3] or similar to base/left/right wrist views as PIL, state pad, dummy_action=32D, deepcopy+merge), predict_action_chunk+postprocess+pending queue (amortizes ~300ms forward), tuned ee-delta bridge (pos*0.05/rot*0.25/grip>0.5->1/-1), complete main() parsing eval_config.json+track_path for RecordingEvaluator will produce real policy actions without error/fallback. Matches intended Pi0 track (LIBERO-pretrained on similar pick/place should yield >0 success on add_condiment/select_* once end-to-end). Decision: Overwrite predict.py with COMPLETE clean non-truncated implementation (exact diagnostic pattern + full agent methods + error recovery only on true exceptions), keep test_preprocess.py, run python test_preprocess.py && python test_obs.py for validation (expect FULL SEQUENTIAL PREPROCESS SUCCEEDED + real policy actions in mock without fallback or image-missing error). Then submit_eval on base ckpt for first non-zero success_rate measurement before LoRA.
